content.full_text:
Pasadena Fire Fighters  
Local No. 809

P.O. BOX 5102  
PASADENA, CALIFORNIA 91107

Kathryn Donavan, General Counsel  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
Sacramento, CA 95814

January 30, 1990

Dear Ms. Donovan,

Several years ago the Pasadena Firefighters Association established a broad based political action committee. With the advent of campaign reform we became somewhat reluctant to use the funds that we had accumulated and have continued to collect. Basically we figured that it would be better to wait and see what happened when the dust from Props 68 & 73 settled. If it has settled sufficiently perhaps you could answer a couple of questions. We are aware of the basic prohibitions and we wondered if aside from the obvious prohibitions and limits, including amounts, transfer and time constraints on campaign funding, if we can do other things such as donating funds to charity and public relations efforts.

In the area of public relations we would like to sponsor school competitions that would heighten community awareness and sensitivity to such problems as crime, drugs, finishing school and developing self esteem. Our plan is to sponsor appropriate competitions in schools and recognize achievement by providing air transportation and lodging in Sacramento where the students could meet their legislators. We would like to have the winners selected from the finalists by local officials. We have arranged for reduced rates from the airlines that service Sacramento from our area.

I spoke to "Kevin" at FPPC and he indicated that what we had in mind was OK. I'm afraid that based on all the prohibitions, constraints and reporting requirements I would like to have a memo indicating that what we have in mind is indeed alright. We have heard so much about what can't be done, we wonder what can be done. Your assistance in this regard will be greatly appreciated.

Sincerely,

John Tennant, President
